answer:
**Manusmṛti (Chapter 8)** construes admissible witnesses primarily through qualities of character, social status, and varṇa, with a strong emphasis on moral and spiritual integrity. Witnesses must be householders with sons, of established lineage, from Kshatriya, Vaiśya, or Śūdra varṇas (not just anyone, except in distress), trustworthy, knowing all dharmas, and non-greedy.[^1] Exclude those connected by wealth/kinship, relatives, enemies, diseased, defamed, kings, artisans, actors, śrotriyas, ascetics, dependents, robbers, intoxicated, insane, etc.[^2] Women testify for women; twice-born for twice-born; Śūdras for Śūdras.[^3] In conflicts, majority prevails, then superior quality, then best twice-born.[^4] Testimony is established by seeing/hearing; truth yields dharma/wealth, falsehood hell.[^5] Questioning invokes spiritual terror: oaths by Brahmin ("Speak"), Kṣatriya ("truth"), etc.; warnings of Brahmin-slayer worlds, merits to dogs, Yama's witness, naked begging, hells.[^6] The Self is ultimate witness; gods/earth/heart/Moon/Sun know conduct.[^7]

**Yājñavalkya Smṛti (Chapter 2)** emphasizes ethical virtues and minimal numbers, with spiritual admonitions. Ideal: ascetics, charitable, good family, truth-tellers, dharma-paramount, upright, with sons/wealth.[^8] At least three, same varṇa/caste (or all for all).[^9] Incompetents: women/children/aged/gamblers/intoxicated/insane/sinners/actors/heretics/forgers/defective senses/outcastes/friends/pecuniary/enemies/thieves/violent/convicted/banished.[^10] Even one dharmic witness approved by both ok; anyone in adultery/theft/assault/violence.[^11] Admonish: false testimony yields sinners'/arsonists'/slayers' worlds, merits lost.[^12] Conflicts: majority, then virtue, most virtuous.[^13] False witnesses fined double dispute (Brahmin banished).[^14]

**Bṛhaspati Smṛti (Chapter 1)** is highly procedural, classifying 12 witness types (subscribed, recorded, concealed, reminded, kinsman, messenger, chance, secondary, present-at-transaction, king, judge, village) with definitions and hierarchies.[^15] Numbers: 9/7/5/4/3/2 Brahmins (never one); single for gambler/stake-seizer/king/judge.[^16] Incompetents detailed (kin, adulterers/drinkers/gamblers/convicted/insane/afflicted/violent/atheists).[^17] Relative strength: numbers > qualities > religiosity > honesty.[^18] Faults impeached timely; proof burden specified; contradictions invalidate.[^19] Less spiritual; focus on court mechanics, summoning/guarding, conflicts with assessors.[^20]

**Most procedural**: Bṛhaspati—elaborate typology (12 kinds), impeachment rules, hierarchies, evidentiary burdens make it systematic/court-focused.[^21]  
**Most spiritual**: Manu—cosmic witnesses (gods/Self/Yama), hell/merit oaths dominate, truth as purification.[^22]
